title: Shocking New AI Just Hit 12 Million Tokens With 1000x Less Compute
summary: Subquadratic unveiled SubQ, a model claiming 12 million token context with subquadratic attention complexity, achieving approximately 1000x reduction in attention compute compared to standard approaches. The model targets enterprise reasoning over full documents, codebases, and legal contracts without relying on retrieval-augmented generation or document chunking.
writeup: The video covers SubQ, a model from Subquadratic that claims a 12 million token context window with roughly 1000x less attention compute than standard approaches. The mechanism is Sparse Selective Attention (SSA), which targets the quadratic cost of attention to make long-context processing practical. The stated payoff: reasoning over full documents, codebases, and contracts directly, without retrieval-augmented generation as a workaround for limited context.
